name: away_with_active_natvent_transition
description: Warm day: nat-vent running at home. User leaves (occupancy_away) at 15:00. Production only changes the setpoint to setback_cool â€” nat-vent continues running. There is no explicit nat-vent exit event on occupancy transition.
notes: Production handle_occupancy_away() does NOT deactivate nat-vent. It only changes the setpoint.
Nat-vent continues until a natural exit condition fires: outdoor rises, comfort floor hit, threshold exceeded.
This scenario verifies: (1) away setback is applied, (2) nat-vent state is NOT prematurely exited.
The assertion that nat-vent is still active after away transition documents production behavior.
Previously this scenario incorrectly asserted nat_vent_exit at the away transition â€” that was

events.note: Morning: indoor 73.5F, outdoor 65F â€” nat-vent favorable
Window opens: nat-vent activates (outdoor 65F < indoor 73.5F, below threshold 77F)
User leaves. Production: only setpoint changes to setback_cool. Nat-vent continues.

assertions.reason: sensor_open: outdoor=65F < indoor=73.5F, indoor > comfort_heat=70F, threshold=77F â€” nat-vent activates
Away transition applies setback_cool=79F. This is the only change production makes.
After away transition, nat-vent state flag must still be True. Production does not exit nat-vent on occupancy change â€” only the setpoint changes. Checks SimState.natural_vent_active directly.

verdict.summary: Away transition changes setpoint only; nat-vent continues running (production behavior)
verdict.observed_behavior: occupancy_away fires -> setback_applied (79F); nat-vent flag remains active
verdict.expected_behavior: Leaving home while nat-vent is active: only setpoint changes. No explicit nat-vent exit event. Nat-vent exits naturally when outdoor conditions change.
